heading: Federal flight deck officer program

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: The Administrator shall establish a program to deputize volunteer pilots of air carriers providing air transportation or intrastate air transportation as Federal law enforcement officers to defend the flight decks of aircraft of such air carriers against acts of criminal violence or air piracy. Such officers shall be known as “Federal flight deck officers”.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish procedural requirements to carry out the program under this section.

children.heading: .—Commencement of program
children.label: 2 .—Commencement of program
children.text: The Administrator shall train and deputize pilots who are qualified to be Federal flight deck officers as Federal flight deck officers under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: The type of firearm to be used by a Federal flight deck officer.

children.label: B
children.text: The type of ammunition to be used by a Federal flight deck officer.

children.label: C
children.text: The standards and training needed to qualify and requalify as a Federal flight deck officer.

children.label: D
children.text: The placement of the firearm of a Federal flight deck officer on board the aircraft to ensure both its security and its ease of retrieval in an emergency.

children.label: E
children.text: An analysis of the risk of catastrophic failure of an aircraft as a result of the discharge (including an accidental discharge) of a firearm to be used in the program into the avionics, electrical systems, or other sensitive areas of the aircraft.

children.label: F
children.text: The division of responsibility between pilots in the event of an act of criminal violence or air piracy if only 1 pilot is a Federal flight deck officer and if both pilots are Federal flight deck officers.

children.label: G
children.text: Procedures for ensuring that the firearm of a Federal flight deck officer does not leave the cockpit if there is a disturbance in the passenger cabin of the aircraft or if the pilot leaves the cockpit for personal reasons.

children.label: H
children.text: Interaction between a Federal flight deck officer and a Federal air marshal on board the aircraft.

children.label: I
children.text: The process for selection of pilots to participate in the program based on their fitness to participate in the program, including whether an additional background check should be required beyond that required by section 44936(a)(1).

children.label: J
children.text: Storage and transportation of firearms between flights, including international flights, to ensure the security of the firearms, focusing particularly on whether such security would be enhanced by requiring storage of the firearm at the airport when the pilot leaves the airport to remain overnight away from the pilot’s base airport.

children.label: K
children.text: Methods for ensuring that security personnel will be able to identify whether a pilot is authorized to carry a firearm under the program.

children.label: L
children.text: Methods for ensuring that pilots (including Federal flight deck officers) will be able to identify whether a passenger is a law enforcement officer who is authorized to carry a firearm aboard the aircraft.

children.label: M
children.text: Any other issues that the Administrator considers necessary.

children.label: N
children.text: The Administrator’s decisions regarding the methods for implementing each of the foregoing procedural requirements shall be subject to review only for abuse of discretion.

children.heading: .—Preference
children.label: 4 .—Preference
children.text: In selecting pilots to participate in the program, the Administrator shall give preference to pilots who are former military or law enforcement personnel.

children.heading: .—Classified information
children.label: 5 .—Classified information
children.text: Notwithstanding but subject to section 40119 of this title, information developed under paragraph (3)(E) shall not be disclosed.section 552 of title 51See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Notice to congress
children.label: 6 .—Notice to congress
children.text: The Administrator shall provide notice to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate after completing the analysis required by paragraph (3)(E).

children.heading: .—Minimization of risk
children.label: 7 .—Minimization of risk
children.text: If the Administrator determines as a result of the analysis under paragraph (3)(E) that there is a significant risk of the catastrophic failure of an aircraft as a result of the discharge of a firearm, the Administrator shall take such actions as may be necessary to minimize that risk.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall only be obligated to provide the training, supervision, and equipment necessary for a pilot to be a Federal flight deck officer under this section at no expense to the pilot or the air carrier employing the pilot.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall base the requirements for the training of Federal flight deck officers under subsection (b) on the training standards applicable to Federal air marshals; except that the Administrator shall take into account the differing roles and responsibilities of Federal flight deck officers and Federal air marshals.

children.label: i
children.text: Training to ensure that the officer achieves the level of proficiency with a firearm required under subparagraph (C)(i).

children.label: ii
children.text: Training to ensure that the officer maintains exclusive control over the officer’s firearm at all times, including training in defensive maneuvers.

children.label: iii
children.text: Training to assist the officer in determining when it is appropriate to use the officer’s firearm and when it is appropriate to use less than lethal force.

children.heading: Training in use of firearms.—
children.label: C Training in use of firearms.—

children.heading: .—Standard
children.label: i .—Standard
children.text: In order to be deputized as a Federal flight deck officer, a pilot must achieve a level of proficiency with a firearm that is required by the Administrator. Such level shall be comparable to the level of proficiency required of Federal air marshals.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: The training of a Federal flight deck officer in the use of a firearm may be conducted by the Administrator or by a firearms training facility.

children.heading: .—Access to training facilities
children.label: II .—Access to training facilities
children.text: The Administrator shall designate additional firearms training facilities located in various regions of the United States for Federal flight deck officers for recurrent and requalifying training relative to the number of such facilities available on the day before such date of enactment.2So in original.2

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall require a Federal flight deck officer to requalify to carry a firearm under the program. Such requalification shall occur at an interval required by the Administrator.

children.heading: .—Use of facilities for requalification
children.label: II .—Use of facilities for requalification
children.text: The Administrator shall allow a Federal flight deck officer to requalify to carry a firearm under the program through training at a Transportation Security Administration-approved firearms training facility utilizing a Transportation Security Administration-approved contractor and a curriculum developed and approved by the Transportation Security Administration.

children.heading: .—Periodic review
children.label: iv .—Periodic review
children.text: The Administrator shall periodically review requalification training intervals and assess whether it is appropriate and sufficient to adjust the time between each requalification training to facilitate continued participation in the program under this section while still maintaining effectiveness of the training, and update the training requirements as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Training review
children.label: D .—Training review
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of the TSA Modernization Act, and biennially thereafter, the Administrator shall review training facilities and training requirements for initial and recurrent training for Federal flight deck officers and evaluate how training requirements, including the length of training, could be streamlined while maintaining the effectiveness of the training, and update the training requirements as appropriate.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator may deputize, as a Federal flight deck officer under this section, a pilot who submits to the Administrator a request to be such an officer and whom the Administrator determines is qualified to be such an officer.

children.label: i
children.text: the pilot is employed by an air carrier;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Administrator determines (in the Administrator’s discretion) that the pilot meets the standards established by the Administrator for being such an officer; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator determines that the pilot has completed the training required by the Administrator.

children.heading: .—Consistency with requirements for certain medical certificates
children.label: B .—Consistency with requirements for certain medical certificates
children.text: In establishing standards under subparagraph (A)(ii), the Administrator may not establish medical or physical standards for a pilot to become a Federal flight deck officer that are inconsistent with or more stringent than the requirements of the Federal Aviation Administration for the issuance of the required airman medical certificate under part 67 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: .—Deputization by other federal agencies
children.label: 3 .—Deputization by other federal agencies
children.text: The Administrator may request another Federal agency to deputize, as Federal flight deck officers under this section, those pilots that the Administrator determines are qualified to be such officers.

children.heading: .—Revocation
children.label: 4 .—Revocation
children.text: The Administrator may (in the Administrator’s discretion) revoke the deputization of a pilot as a Federal flight deck officer if the Administrator finds that the pilot is no longer qualified to be such an officer.

children.heading: .—Transfer from inactive to active status
children.label: 5 .—Transfer from inactive to active status
children.text: In accordance with any applicable Transportation Security Administration appeals processes, a pilot deputized as a Federal flight deck officer who moves to inactive status may return to active status upon successful completion of a recurrent training program administered within program guidelines.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Pilots participating in the program under this section shall not be eligible for compensation from the Federal Government for services provided as a Federal flight deck officer. The Federal Government and air carriers shall not be obligated to compensate a pilot for participating in the program or for the pilot’s training or qualification and requalification to carry firearms under the program.

children.heading: .—Facilitation of training
children.label: 2 .—Facilitation of training
children.text: An air carrier shall permit a pilot seeking to be deputized as a Federal flight deck officer or a Federal flight deck officer to take a reasonable amount of leave to participate in initial, recurrent, or requalification training, as applicable, for the program. Leave required under this paragraph may be provided without compensation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall authorize a Federal flight deck officer to carry a firearm while engaged in providing air transportation or intrastate air transportation. Notwithstanding subsection (c)(1), the officer may purchase a firearm and carry that firearm aboard an aircraft of which the officer is the pilot in accordance with this section if the firearm is of a type that may be used under the program.

children.heading: .—Preemption
children.label: 2 .—Preemption
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal or State law, a Federal flight deck officer, whenever necessary to participate in the program, may carry a firearm in any State and from 1 State to another State.

children.heading: .—Carrying firearms outside united states
children.label: 3 .—Carrying firearms outside united states
children.text: In consultation with the Secretary of State, the Administrator may take such action as may be necessary to ensure that a Federal flight deck officer may carry a firearm in a foreign country whenever necessary to participate in the program.

children.heading: .—Consistency with federal air marshal program
children.label: 4 .—Consistency with federal air marshal program
children.text: The Administrator shall harmonize, to the extent practicable and in a manner that does not jeopardize existing Federal air marshal agreements, the policies relating to the carriage of firearms on international flights by Federal flight deck officers with the policies of the Federal air marshal program for carrying firearms on such flights and carrying out the duties of a Federal flight deck officer, notwithstanding Annex 17 of the International Civil Aviation Organization.

children.heading: .—Authority To Use Force
children.label: g .—Authority To Use Force
children.text: Notwithstanding section 44903(d), the Administrator shall prescribe the standards and circumstances under which a Federal flight deck officer may use, while the program under this section is in effect, force (including lethal force) against an individual in the defense of the flight deck of an aircraft in air transportation or intrastate air transportation.

children.heading: .—Liability of air carriers
children.label: 1 .—Liability of air carriers
children.text: An air carrier shall not be liable for damages in any action brought in a Federal or State court arising out of a Federal flight deck officer’s use of or failure to use a firearm.

children.heading: .—Liability of federal flight deck officers
children.label: 2 .—Liability of federal flight deck officers
children.text: A Federal flight deck officer shall not be liable for damages in any action brought in a Federal or State court arising out of the acts or omissions of the officer in defending the flight deck of an aircraft against acts of criminal violence or air piracy unless the officer is guilty of gross negligence or willful misconduct.

children.heading: .—Liability of federal government
children.label: 3 .—Liability of federal government
children.text: For purposes of an action against the United States with respect to an act or omission of a Federal flight deck officer in defending the flight deck of an aircraft, the officer shall be treated as an employee of the Federal Government under chapter 171 of title 28, relating to tort claims procedure.
shall revoke the deputization of the Federal flight deck officer responsible for that firearm if the Administrator determines that the discharge was attributable to the negligence of the officer; and

if the Administrator determines that a shortcoming in standards, training, or procedures was responsible for the accidental discharge, may temporarily suspend the program until the shortcoming is corrected.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Authority of Air Carriers
children.label: j .—Limitation on Authority of Air Carriers

children.text: prohibit a Federal flight deck officer from piloting an aircraft operated by the air carrier; or
terminate the employment of a Federal flight deck officer, solely on the basis of his or her volunteering for or participating in the program under this section.

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: k .—Applicability
children.text: This section shall not apply to air carriers operating under part 135 of title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, and to pilots employed by such carriers to the extent that such carriers and pilots are covered by section 135.119 of such title or any successor to such section.

children.heading: .—Administrator
children.label: 1 .—Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Transportation Security Administration.

children.heading: .—Air transportation
children.label: 2 .—Air transportation
children.text: The term “air transportation” includes all-cargo air transportation.

children.heading: .—Firearms training facility
children.label: 3 .—Firearms training facility
children.text: The term “firearms training facility” means a private or government-owned gun range approved by the Administrator to provide recurrent or requalification training, as applicable, for the program, utilizing a Transportation Security Administration-approved contractor and a curriculum developed and approved by the Transportation Security Administration.

children.heading: .—Pilot
children.label: 4 .—Pilot
children.text: The term “pilot” means an individual who has final authority and responsibility for the operation and safety of the flight or any other flight deck crew member.
